shots.speech_text: Imagine Silanol (Si–OH) surface density on fully hydroxylated amorph. That single number is the key to understanding Silica surface chemistry at the mineral–water interface — one of the most extraordinary phenomena in the known universe.

shots.speech_text: The key measurement: Silanol (Si–OH) surface density on fully hydroxylated amorphous silica: 4.6–4.9 OH/nm² (Zhuravlev model, 2000); Si–O bond length 1.62 Å; Si–O–Si bond angle ~144°. Results like this, produced by NIST Material Measurement Laboratory, transformed our understanding of Silica surface chemistry at the mineral–water interface.
shots.visual_direction: EVIDENCE SHOT: [Data visualisation, telescope imagery, or archival footage of the key discovery moment]. Institution logos/credits if available. Measurement values as lower-third text overlay.

shots.speech_text: Silica surface chemistry at the mineral–water interface is not just a phenomenon at the far edge of science. It connects directly to questions about the fabric of reality. The a has implications that reach far beyond the laboratory.

shots.speech_text: We are only beginning to understand Silica surface chemistry at the mineral–water interface. And perhaps that's exactly the point — the universe keeps its most intricate secrets just within reach, if we keep looking.

provenance_card.title: Silica surface chemistry at the mineral–water interface
provenance_card.key_measurement: Silanol (Si–OH) surface density on fully hydroxylated amorphous silica: 4.6–4.9 OH/nm² (Zhuravlev model, 2000); Si–O bond length 1.62 Å; Si–O–Si bond angle ~144°
institutions: Russian Academy of Sciences Institute of Physical Chemistry (Zhuravlev group)

guardrails: HELIX is voiceover only — no on-screen host, no synthetic presenter, no avatar.
All quantitative claims must include units and citation.
Uncertainty must be stated where present (e.g. 'approximately', 'within a margin of').
Do not present theoretical models as confirmed fact without qualification.
Do not fabricate measurements, paper titles, DOIs, or institutional affiliations.
Maintain wonder without hyperbole — the phenomena are extraordinary enough.
No speculation presented as established science.
HELIX does not cover history, biography, or language — those are DAVID's domain.
